item.title: node.js EventEmitter example
item.description: This post is a part of the Project Restore, with the primary aim of excavating my previous writings. The material contained herein may not hold practical relevance and is most likely completely useless. What is it? Node.js comes with a core module called events. And EventEmitter is a class on the events object. EventEmitter has stability of 4 which means that API is frozen. In other words things in events are unlikely to change.

item.title: Express 4 routing guide
item.description: Introduction In this article we will look at routing with Express. Express is the most popular web framework built on node.js. Popularity of Express can be judged by looking at the number of downloads from npm registry which tells us that Express was downloaded more than 2.5 miliion times last month. Other than websites, Express also makes it easy to develop RESTFul services. What is routing Routing is one of the fundamental building blocks for any web framework.

item.title: Upgrade node.js on mac
item.description: This post is a part of the Project Restore, with the primary aim of excavating my previous writings. The material contained herein may not hold practical relevance and is most likely completely useless. One way to upgrade node.js on your machine is to download the latest installer from node.js website. The other is to update node.js from CLI which is my preferred way. To upgrade node.js we should have n installed.

item.title: Test REST APIs with Frisby
item.description: This post is a part of the Project Restore, with the primary aim of excavating my previous writings. The material contained herein may not hold practical relevance and is most likely completely useless. Introduction As software developers we strive for exellence in our craft. This means writing software we and our clients are proud of. One way to ensure such exellence is to test everything. Even better is to write tests which can test our code for us commonly known as unit tests or end-to-end tests.

item.title: List of node.js core modules
item.description: This post is a part of the Project Restore, with the primary aim of excavating my previous writings. The material contained herein may not hold practical relevance and is most likely completely useless. node.js comes with many core modules which are compiled to binary when node.js is installed. This is a list of node.js core modules with links to documentation. I find it good to have a handy list of things, hence this post.

item.title: 5 npm commands recommended for node.js developers
item.description: Search for packages npm allows you to search the npm registry from command line. When you execute the command for the first time it builds an index. If you are searching for package(s) which have something to do with png file you can run $ npm search png This will find all packages which have png in its metadata. Find owner of a package npm owner command can be used to find owner(s) of a package.

item.title: Node.js - Make npm work behind proxy
item.description: This post is a part of the Project Restore, with the primary aim of excavating my previous writings. The material contained herein may not hold practical relevance and is most likely completely useless. Installing node.js modules in a corporate environment can be problematic if your connection to the world is via a proxy. I experienced this while setting up a node.js environment at one of my client’s office. I always use my personal machine which is a mac but often I have to work with folks who use Windows.
